Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Why are you introducing a new "mgt" type?  mkfs.lustre does not have any such option, right?

And why does the test treat MGS and MGT like they are different roles?  What is the idea behind that?

Patch Set 5:

> (1 comment)
 > 
 > Why are you introducing a new "mgt" type?  mkfs.lustre does not
 > have any such option, right?
 > 
 > And why does the test treat MGS and MGT like they are different
 > roles?  What is the idea behind that?

MGT was pre-existing in ldev. I recently added MGS as a valid option. MGT is equivalent to MGS from my understanding, I can introduce a change to make them behave as equivalents.

Patch Set 5:

Yes, that is one way to go.

But It is basically just a way just because ldev allows MGS or MGT in the device name, that is not an argument on its own that we need to invent an "mgt" type that does not currently exist in lustre.

If you want to point out more code examples, I could be convinced, but right now I am leaning towards it being best not to have an mgt type until the lustre commands themselves support such a thing.
Uploaded patch set 6.

Looks like there is a case where a user would not get the target(s) they asked for.
Patch Set 8: Code-Review-1

Patch Set 8:

> (1 comment)
 > 
 > Looks like there is a case where a user would not get the target(s)
 > they asked for.

ldev -R MGT is currently not considered valid. Chris had suggested removing the MGT role. If the user does "ldev -R MGS", the lquake-MGT0000 label will be returned. If that is not sufficient, then a discussion regarding MGS vs MGT may need to happen.

Patch Set 8:

(1 comment)

Joe,

I suggest you just remove MGT.  See details below.

-Olaf

parents.subject: LU-8357 nodemap: reclassify nodemap requires active conf lock

commit.subject: LU-8055 ldev: Introduced role filtering
commit.message:
LU-8055 ldev: Introduced role filtering

Output can now be filtered by using -R <role>
or --role <role> as command line parameters,
where role is a string such as "mdt", "ost",
or "mgs".

Also removed MGT as a possible node type
in ldev.conf.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=conf-sanity
Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Di Natale <dinatale2@llnl.gov>
Change-Id: I488ac702edf0cc7c08020b289dcba040706181b4
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/20041
Reviewed-by: Olaf Faaland-LLNL <faaland1@llnl.gov>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Christopher J. Morrone <morrone2@llnl.gov>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
